claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects project file mutations before and after full-tool Claude agent execution and blocks PRD readiness when violations occur or evidence lacks required scope declarations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot walks project files, computes SHA-256 digests, excludes allowed roots, and returns a digest map. diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after maps to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: Core boundary detection implementation identifies mutations via file content hashing.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes boundary snapshot before agent execution, takes snapshot after, diffs to detect changes, sets project_mutation to violated if changes exist, and generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that set prd_ready to false.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement flow proves boundary audit actively blocks PRD readiness on mutations.

evidence.summary: Execution policy declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots restricted to output_dir only.
evidence.why: Policy documents the boundary contract that the audit enforces.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules explicitly forbid modifying target project files and require evidence scope declarations on every record.
evidence.why: Evidence agent instructions prevent mutations and mandate evidence provenance.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents unverified evidence from being accepted.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type, ensuring project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts.
evidence.why: Classification enforces evidence requirements for existing project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators or have only non-project scopes.
evidence.why: Enforcement prevents assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates blockers when evidence records omit required scope field.
evidence.why: Validation prevents ambiguous evidence sources from passing.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns explorer, cross-checker, verifier for cross_check policy to force independent verification.
evidence.why: Cross-check protocol prevents single-agent errors on high-risk claims.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false.
evidence.why: Protocol contract requires evidence agents to be read-only.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude, codex, or custom provider commands with configurable tool profiles for evidence agent execution.
evidence.why: Provider abstraction enables full-tool or boundary-restricted agent spawning.

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker in result.readiness.blockers.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement actually blocks PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations and gitignored mutations outside excluded dirs, with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary works with git repos and gitignored files.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts in allowed_write_roots, with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary works without git and respects allowed roots.

evidence.summary: Tests verify external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and scope-less evidence is blocked.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves evidence scope enforcement prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: Previous dispatch shows boundary.project_mutation: clean with zero changes, readiness.prd_ready: true, and readiness.blockers: [] after all three agents completed with project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Real execution artifact proves boundary audit passed with no mutations detected.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect mutations in non-git repos due to filesystem timing issues, agents could corrupt projects without blockers.
If claude provider ignores tool profile restrictions at runtime despite policy declaration, full-tool agents might mutate projects.
If evidence scope classification heuristics misclassify external URLs as project-sourced due to path patterns, unverified claims could become facts.

result.notes: Evidence confirms boundary audit exists, is tested across git/non-git scenarios, and blocks PRD readiness on mutations or missing scope. 16 project-scoped evidence records with exact line numbers. Previous successful dispatch artifact proves clean execution. Cross-check verification recommended to confirm no detection gaps.
